life_history: Mason has spent eleven months in Europe working on a significant project that involved creating a website and conducting interviews. This experience has been pivotal for him, and he is eager to showcase the results of his efforts. However, he has a history of procrastination and anxiety, which have impacted his ability to manage tasks effectively. Despite implementing structured routines and strategies learned from a course, he continues to struggle with feelings of inadequacy and the pressure to be productive.
core_belief_description: Helpless: I am incompetent. I am out of control. I am a failure, loser.
intermediate_beliefs: If I'm not productive or not doing anything significant, I'm wasting my life. I must keep up with the schedule. If I stay idle, then I'm failing.
I must always have something to show for my choices, actions, and efforts; otherwise, I'm a failure. I must always make the right choice.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: I just keep procrastinating and can never catch up with the schedule. Failure is imminent. My project is going to fail due to my incompetency.
Having options is paralyzing due to the pressure to make the perfect choice. I cannot make the right choice; I'm a failure.
coping_strategies: Using structured daily routines with set times for activities such as reading, playing guitar, and going to the gym.
Setting reminders for tasks and trying to be present in the moment to reduce idle time.
Engaging in avoidance or procrastination when overwhelmed by decision-making.

cognitive_models.situation: Working on a project that is due soon and has increased visibility due to recent sociopolitical events.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: I need to finish this asap. I have not finished the work that I need to put online; I'm behind schedule. I will fail on this project.
cognitive_models.behavior: Rushes to work on the project, foregoes sleep, endures stress and extreme anxiety.

cognitive_models.situation: Thinking about life choices and direction while dealing with ambiguity and too many options.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: I can't make the wrong decision. I might fail or choose incorrectly, which could have serious implications for my life.
cognitive_models.behavior: Becomes paralyzed by options and fears making decisions, may engage in rumination.
